story.text: As the loop, I need a commit to refuse when live HEAD is not the branch the session believes it is on — because in the shared-tree fleet a parallel session can move the worktree's branch between a session reading HEAD and committing, and the work lands on the wrong branch (this session's own collision). The repo's law is that the fix is structural, not 'verify by hand': a session's self-discipline is the unreliable component. The git pen already computes the current branch and refuses detached/trunk commits; this adds one assertion — `--on <branch>` — and refuses when it disagrees with live HEAD. The cheapest structural closer: the sheath, not the whole session gateway.

briefing.headline: the HEAD-intent guard: a commit refuses when HEAD moved under the session
briefing.value: .claude/skills/branch-ritual/git.py's commit verb gains --on <branch>: the session asserts the branch it believes it is on, and the pen refuses (a `report`, reason named) if live HEAD differs. Stateless and per-invocation — explicit intent, nothing another session can race. Omitting --on is unchanged (backward compatible). Turns the exact branch collision this session hit into a clean deny.
briefing.why_now: This session committed eval work onto a parallel session's branch because the shared worktree's HEAD switched under it. Root cause (session-gateway.proposal.md §2): one HEAD per tree makes 'the current branch' shared mutable global state a session treats as private. This is proposal increment #1 — the cheapest structural fix.
briefing.if_accepted: A session can assert its branch and be protected from the shared-tree race; the collision becomes a refusal with the fix named. Built and committed in an isolated worktree, dogfooding the insulation principle the proposal names — and the guard is used for its own landing commit (--on claude/head-intent-guard).
briefing.if_rejected: The shared-tree HEAD race stays open; sessions keep relying on hand-verifying their branch (the unreliable component), and the collision recurs.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and fail-safe: --on is opt-in (omitting it preserves every existing caller), the check is a pure comparison, and reverting is deleting one function + one flag. It refuses only on a genuine mismatch.
briefing.mechanism: git.py: head_intent_refusal(branch, expected) returns a reason when expected is given and != live branch (naming detached HEAD too); cmd_commit calls it before commit_refusal; the commit verb gains --on. tests/test_git_pen_head_intent.py: unit-tests the pure function (wrong refuses + is named, matching passes, omitted passes, detached named) and drives the live pen as a subprocess (wrong-branch refuses with a HEAD-intent reason; right-branch does not trip the guard) — the §10 teeth being that a constant-pass guard fails the wrong-branch case and a constant-refuse guard fails the right-branch case. SKILL.md documents the flag. Done-line 0118.
